Data Visualization · Assignment 4

Tree & Graph
Visualization

Dataset 1  Animal Kingdom Taxonomy
Dataset 2  MUSAE Facebook Page-Page Network
Library  NetworkX · Matplotlib
Layouts  4 total (2 per dataset)
01

Datasets

Tree Dataset
Animal Kingdom Taxonomy
SourceKaggle — hasanarcas/animal-kingdom-taxonomy
Records960 animal species
NodesTaxonomic ranks: Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species
EdgesParent-child relationships (e.g. Animalia → Chordata → Mammalia)
Why treeStrictly hierarchical, no cycles, single root (Animalia)
Graph Dataset
MUSAE Facebook Page-Page Network
SourceKaggle — rozemberczki/musae-facebook-pagepage-network
Records22,470 nodes · 171,002 edges
NodesVerified Facebook pages (news, brands, public figures)
EdgesMutual likes — if Page A and Page B liked each other
Why graphHas cycles, bidirectional links, no hierarchy — general graph
02

Tree Visualization — Animal Kingdom Taxonomy

Layout 1
Hierarchical (Top-Down)
Nodes arranged level-by-level from root to leaves, clearly showing the parent-child taxonomy structure.
Layout 2
Radial (Shell)
Root placed at center, branches radiate outward in concentric rings — ideal for seeing all taxonomy levels at once.
03

Graph Visualization — Facebook Page-Page Network

Layout 1
Spring (Force-Directed)
Edges act as springs — highly connected hub pages naturally cluster together in the center.
Layout 2
Kamada-Kawai
Minimizes difference between graph-theoretic and Euclidean distances — produces a geometrically balanced layout.
04

Summary

#DatasetTypeLayoutKey Insight
1 Animal Kingdom Taxonomy Tree Hierarchical Top-Down Clearly shows the 3-level hierarchy from Kingdom → Phylum → Class
2 Animal Kingdom Taxonomy Tree Radial Shell Kingdom at center radiates outward; good for comparing all branches
3 Facebook Page-Page Network Graph Spring Force-Directed Hub pages cluster naturally; reveals high-degree nodes at center
4 Facebook Page-Page Network Graph Kamada-Kawai Geometrically balanced; separates dense and sparse clusters clearly
